steps.title: Identify the structural pattern
steps.content: The passage introduces the Federalist Papers—what they are, who wrote them, when and where they were published, and why they mattered—then pivots to address what about them is not completely known: the authorship of certain essays.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content: The text first establishes facts about a collection of essays, then addresses something not completely known about those essays—the authorship of certain ones is uncertain. No. 6 was definitely Hamilton's, but No. 62 could have been written by either Hamilton or Madison. The other choices either mischaracterize the authorship question, focus only on publication details, or invent authorial disagreement. That matches choice D — D is correct.
